rationale: The cited paper develops a correction account (reflexive vs. deliberative processing) in persuasion judgments, and the citing paper also studies persuasion spillovers with affect-as-information boundary conditions (e.g., monitoring feelings, source salience) that could rely on that mechanism-level prior work. Given this conceptual overlap and no clear sign of a purely incidental citation (the only match is DOI metadata, with no in-text context), this pair is not safe to drop as mention-only.
